https://github.com/tukaani-project/xz/security/advisories/GHSA-x872-m794-cxhv"},{"url":"https://security-tracker.debian.org/tracker/CVE-2026-34743","title":"CVE-2026-34743","content":"The information below is based on the following data on fixed versions."},{"url":"https://feedly.com/cve/CVE-2026-34743","title":"CVE-2026-34743 - Exploits & Severity - Feedly","content":"XZ Utils versions prior to 5.8.3 contain a heap-based buffer overflow vulnerability in the lzma_index_decoder() function.
When decoding an Index that contains no Records, the resulting lzma_index is left in an improper state.
A subsequent call to lzma_index_append() would allocate insufficient memory, leading to a buffer overflow condition.
An attacker could exploit this vulnerability by providing specially crafted compressed data with an empty Index to applications using XZ Utils.
This could result in a buffer overflow that may lead to denial of service through application crashes or potential code execution, depending on the application's memory layout and protections.
Affected version in inventory: azl3_xz_5.4.4-2 (Microsoft).
Inventory systems running XZ Utils versions prior to 5.8.3 and apply patches immediately.
Feedly found the first article mentioning CVE-2026-34743.
Feedly estimated the CVSS as HIGH based on the CVE details, attack complexity, and exploit information.
Detection for the vulnerability has been added to Nessus (305980 306017)
See 59 more articles and social media posts"},{"url":"https://tukaani.org/xz/index-append-overflow.html","title":"CVE-2026-34743: Buffer overflow in lzma_index_append()","content":"CVE-2026-34743: Buffer overflow in lzma_index_append()
The lzma_index functions are rarely used by applications directly.
In the few applications that do use these functions, the combination of function calls required to trigger this bug are unlikely to exist, because there typically is no reason to append Records to a decoded lzma_index.
The bug was fixed in XZ Utils 5.8.3."},{"url":"https://www.tenable.com/plugins/nessus/304496","title":null,"content":"The Linux/Unix host has one or more packages installed with a vulnerability that the vendor indicates will not be patched.
- XZ Utils provide a general-purpose data-compression library plus command-line tools.
This issue has been patched in version 5.8.3.
Note that Nessus relies on the presence of the package as reported by the vendor."}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"CVE-2026-34743 xz utils lzma_index_append affected versions discoverer report","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.sentinelone.com/vulnerability-database/cve-2026-34743","title":"CVE-2026-34743: XZ Utils Buffer Overflow Vulnerability","content":"# CVE-2026-34743: XZ Utils Buffer Overflow Vulnerability. CVE-2026-34743 is a buffer overflow vulnerability in XZ Utils affecting lzma\\_index\\_decoder() function. The vulnerability exists in the lzma\\_index\\_decoder() function when decoding an Index that contains no Records. When this condition occurs, the resulting lzma\\_index structure is left in an invalid state where a subsequent call to lzma\\_index\\_append() allocates insufficient memory, leading to a heap buffer overflow. > Applications using XZ Utils that decode empty Index structures and subsequently append Records may be vulnerable to heap buffer overflow, potentially leading to denial of service or memory corruption. When an application subsequently calls lzma\\_index\\_append() after decoding an empty Index, the function allocates a buffer based on the incorrect prealloc value of zero, resulting in insufficient memory allocation and a subsequent heap buffer overflow when data is written. The root cause is a missing boundary check in the preallocation logic within src/liblzma/common/index.c. When lzma\\_index\\_decoder() is called with records == 0, the code failed to set a safe default value for i->prealloc, leaving it at zero.","score":0.8943568,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://vulners.com/cve/CVE-2026-34743","title":"CVE-2026-34743 - vulnerability database","content":"Apr 2, 2026 — XZ Utils contains a vulnerability (CVE-2026-34743) in lzma_index_append() when decoding an empty index with lzma_index_decoder(),","score":0.87113565,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://feedly.com/cve/CVE-2026-34743","title":"CVE-2026-34743 - Exploits & Severity","content":"Apr 2, 2026 — Summary.
